title: Swap-vs-Recompute Boundary Experiment
problem: The CAAE/KVict controller needs empirical boundaries for when swap beats recompute.
objective: Design an experiment that maps cost regimes across prompt length, PCIe load and GPU utilization.
observations: Prior traces identify recompute/offload boundary as falsifiable horizon.
deductions: If the boundary is hardware-dependent, a single benchmark point is not enough.
If both PCIe and GPU load vary, the experiment needs a grid.
Therefore map the decision surface and fit a simple predictor.
abductive_leaps: Use boundary mapping to calibrate online controller thresholds.
mechanism_steps: sweep sequence length and generation length
minimal_test: Run forced-policy grid and compare predicted cheaper action to measured cheaper action.

provenance.curation_note: Gold-candidate record synthesized from project-specific traces, summaries, and notes; should be human-reviewed before public release.

title: Memory Router Curriculum Experiment
problem: The attention-memory router collapse needs a clean test of imitation and anti-collapse regularizers.
objective: Design an ablation that distinguishes architecture failure from training-policy collapse.
observations: Attention memory traces include B=10 fail and oracle sanity pass.
deductions: If oracle succeeds and learned policy fails, repair should target optimization.
If imitation helps only at high budget, capacity also matters.
Therefore cross budget and curriculum ablations are needed.
abductive_leaps: Use routing-action metrics as primary diagnostic, not just task accuracy.
minimal_test: Run 4 training variants across B=10/B=50 and compare accuracy plus routing-action rates.

provenance.curation_note: Gold-candidate record synthesized from project-specific traces, summaries, and notes; should be human-reviewed before public release.

title: Layered Context Ablation
problem: The four-tier context architecture needs evidence that each tier contributes distinct value.
objective: Design an ablation for raw tokens, structured state, episodic retrieval and cold archive.
observations: Layered Context README and Temporal Compression notes provide tier structure.
deductions: If a tier does not improve accuracy or reduce stale facts, it may be unnecessary.
If tiers interact, single-drop ablations reveal contribution.
Therefore evaluate no-tier and leave-one-tier-out variants.
abductive_leaps: Prove the architecture by removing parts.
mechanism_steps: measure accuracy, stale facts and token budget
failure_modes: query set biased toward intended strengths
minimal_test: Full vs no Tier1/2/3/validity filters on held-out changing-world tasks.
source_basis: fine_tune_dataset.zip: Layered Context README; personal_context: temporal memory work.

provenance.curation_note: Gold-candidate record synthesized from project-specific traces, summaries, and notes; should be human-reviewed before public release.

title: Circuit-LM Depth Generalization Sweep
problem: Circuit-LM needs evidence that stack/PDA features solve depth generalization better than PPM/FSM.
objective: Design a synthetic and code-like benchmark sweep over nesting depth.
observations: Project memory records PDA-2ph improved with depth while PPM degraded.
deductions: If stack features are useful, their advantage should grow with depth.
If they only memorize shallow patterns, held-out deeper depth fails.
Therefore depth sweep is the right falsification test.
abductive_leaps: Use extrapolation depth as the core metric.
mechanism_steps: measure next-token and valid sequence rate
minimal_test: Train on depth ≤N and test on N+1..N+K plus small code snippets.
source_basis: personal_context: Circuit-LM experiments and depth results.

provenance.curation_note: Gold-candidate record synthesized from project-specific traces, summaries, and notes; should be human-reviewed before public release.

title: HGSEL Routing Collapse Benchmark
problem: HGSEL/SRMoE routing needs a benchmark that separates load balance from real specialization.
objective: Design a benchmark for collapse, duplication and specialization quality.
constraints: measure expert load and expert behavior
observations: HGSEL and SRMoE traces emphasize routing collapse, local/global routing and production benchmarks.
deductions: If experts are balanced but identical, entropy alone is misleading.
If specialization is real, expert assignment should correlate with task modes.
Therefore benchmark must measure both utilization and specialization.
abductive_leaps: Use known latent task modes to score expert specialization.
mechanism_steps: train dense, learned-router and local-global variants
measure mutual information between expert and task mode
minimal_test: Evaluate across seeds and router temperature schedules.
source_basis: fine_tune_dataset.zip: HGSEL/SRMoE notes; personal_context: HGSEL phase plan.

title: Invention-Trace LoRA Evaluation
problem: A deduction dataset should prove it improves invention behavior rather than just JSON formatting.
objective: Design an eval comparing base model, success-only fine-tune and mixed-trace fine-tune.
observations: Current dataset mixture includes successes, failures, repairs, experiments and productization.
deductions: If the model only learns schema, content quality will not improve.
If mixed traces teach taste, it should detect contradictions and propose tests better than success-only.
Therefore eval must include both generation and critique tasks.
abductive_leaps: Evaluate invention as a process skill.
mechanism_steps: score with rubric and human review
minimal_test: Train small adapters on success-only and full mixture; score blind prompts with critic + human spot check.
source_basis: current user request and prior deduction dataset discussion.
